title: Philosophical Discussion
description: Vocabulary and frameworks for discussing ethics, meaning, identity, and free will.
summary_pt_br: Frameworks filosóficos: utilitarismo (maior bem), deontologia (dever), ética de virtude (caráter).

scenes.subtitle_en: Vocabulary and frameworks for discussing ethics, meaning, identity, and free will.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Frameworks filosóficos: utilitarismo (maior bem), deontologia (dever), ética de virtude (caráter).

scenes.text_en: Vocabulary and frameworks for discussing ethics, meaning, identity, and free will. Focus: philosophical discussion. From a utilitarian perspective, the action maximizes welfare.
scenes.text_pt: Frameworks filosóficos: utilitarismo (maior bem), deontologia (dever), ética de virtude (caráter). Foco: philosophical discussion. Do ponto de vista utilitarista, a ação maximiza o bem-estar.

scenes.title: Philosophical Discussion
scenes.body_en: Frameworks: UTILITARIAN (greatest good). DEONTOLOGICAL (duty-based). VIRTUE ETHICS (character). Hedged language: 'It might be argued', 'From a utilitarian perspective', 'Kant would respond that...'
scenes.body_pt: Frameworks filosóficos: utilitarismo (maior bem), deontologia (dever), ética de virtude (caráter).

examples.en: From a utilitarian perspective, the action maximizes welfare.
examples.pt: Do ponto de vista utilitarista, a ação maximiza o bem-estar.

examples.en: A Kantian would argue that lying violates moral duty.
examples.pt: Um kantiano argumentaria que mentir viola o dever moral.

examples.en: Virtue ethics asks not what to do, but who to be.
examples.pt: A ética das virtudes pergunta não o que fazer, mas quem ser.

exercise.question: Complete: "From a ___ perspective, the action maximizes welfare."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "From a utilitarian perspective, the action maximizes welfare."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Do ponto de vista utilitarista, a ação maximiza o bem-estar.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: A Kantian would argue that lying violates moral duty.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "A Kantian would argue that lying violates moral duty."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Um kantiano argumentaria que mentir viola o dever moral.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Philosophical Discussion — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre philosophical discussion. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: We maximize the greatest good for the greatest number.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: A Kantian rejects using people as means.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How does virtue ethics reframe the question?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It asks what a wise person would do.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Lying to save a friend tests every framework.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Which view do you find most convincing?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Virtue ethics, because character precedes action.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Philosophy in English is the grammar of thinking slowly.

scenes.title: The ethics seminar
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize the three frameworks discussed. Use at least three philosophical terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In the small seminar room, Kumar opened the week's question: when is it ethical to lie?

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: From a utilitarian perspective, a lie that maximizes welfare could be ethically justified without contradiction.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: A Kantian would respond that duty-based ethics forbid lying regardless of the consequences we expect.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Virtue ethics reframes the question entirely — it asks who you become by telling that particular lie.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: No framework answers every dilemma, but each illuminates a different aspect of our moral autonomy.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Philosophy, Kumar repeated, is the rare subject where not answering is sometimes the most honest answer available.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Philosophical Discussion". Vocabulary and frameworks for discussing ethics, meaning, identity, and free will. From a utilitarian perspective, the action maximizes welfare. A Kantian would argue that lying violates moral duty. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Philosophical Discussion". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
